ingredients.prep: or smoked turkey neck/wing for a lighter option
optional; small amount for Creole-style depth
14-15 oz; optional, use if you like tomato-forward Creole beans
add to taste near the end (smoked meats are salty)
or water; add more as needed

steps.text: Soak beans (preferred): Place beans in a large bowl, cover with about 8 cups water, and soak 8–12 hours. Drain and rinse. Quick-soak option: cover beans with water, boil 2 minutes, remove from heat, cover 1 hour, then drain and rinse.
Brown sausage: Heat oil in a heavy pot over medium-high heat. Add sausage and cook until browned. Transfer sausage to a plate; leave drippings in the pot.
Sweat the trinity: Reduce heat to medium. Add onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened, scraping up browned bits. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Optional tomato depth: If using tomato paste, stir it in and cook 1 minute. If using diced tomatoes, add them now and cook 1–2 minutes.

Simmer: Add drained beans, ham hock, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, paprika, Creole seasoning, black pepper, Worcestershire, and enough stock to cover beans by 1–2 inches. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Cook until creamy: Simmer partially covered until beans are tender and the liquid thickens, stirring occasionally and adding more stock as needed to keep beans covered.
Mash for texture: Remove ham hock. Shred any meat and return to the pot. Mash a portion of beans against the side/bottom of the pot to create a creamy base, then stir to combine.
Return sausage and season: Stir browned sausage back in and simmer 10–15 minutes. Taste and add salt as needed (start small). Add hot sauce to taste.
Serve: Spoon red beans over hot cooked rice. Garnish with green onions and parsley.
